title: Data Engineer | Kjell & Company
description:
**Data Engineer**

**THE OPPORTUNITY**


At **Kjell & Company**, we are looking for a **Data Engineer** who wants to do more than just build pipelines — someone who wants to shape how data drives the business forward.


Our data landscape is evolving — and this is where you come in. As a Data Engineer with us, you will play a key role in transforming our hybrid environment into a modern, scalable, cloud-first platform built on Microsoft Fabric. You’ll work hands-on with both legacy and modern technologies, ensuring reliable and high-quality data across the business closely enabling reporting and analytics within the BI domain.


This is a role for someone who combines strong technical skills with curiosity and drive — and who wants real impact on how we build, structure, and scale our data platform going forward.

**YOUR KEY RESPONSIBILITIES**

* Develop, maintain, and optimize data pipelines across legacy and modern platforms
* Build and evolve solutions in Microsoft Fabric (Data Factory & Notebooks)
* Build and support scalable data models and semantic layers for reporting (e.g. Power BI, DAX)
* Drive the transition to a scalable medallion architecture
* Integrate data from multiple sources (SQL, APIs, SFTP, Delta Share)
* Contribute to CI/CD, DevOps practices, and continuous improvement of workflows

**WE BELIEVE YOU ARE**


You are a highly motivated Data Engineer who takes pride in building scalable, reliable solutions that create real business impact. You combine a structured and detail-oriented mindset with a proactive approach, and you enjoy taking ownership from idea to implementation. You thrive in a collaborative environment, challenge existing ways of working, and contribute to raising the bar for how data engineering is done.

* Solid experience as a Data Engineer in a hybrid environment, ideally within the Microsoft ecosystem
* Strong programming and querying skills in Python/PySpark and SQL
* Proven experience building and optimizing modern data pipelines and workflows
* Experience working with version control, CI/CD, and DevOps-oriented practices
* Experience working with data modeling and enabling semantic layers for reporting (e.g. Power BI)
* Fluent in both Swedish and English

**WHY KJELL & COMPANY**


Be part of a company where data drives real business impact. At Kjell & Company, you’ll work hands-on in a cloud-first transformation, combining modern and legacy platforms while influencing how we build and scale our data capabilities — in a collaborative environment where ownership matters.

**OTHER INFORMATION**


Please note that a background check may be conducted as part of the recruitment process. This applies to all final candidates.

**APPLY TODAY**


We review applications continuously — we look forward to hearing from you.

title: Data Engineer
description:
**About SCRL**
--------------


SCRL is a mobile-first design platform built for social media and used by millions of creators worldwide.


We're a team based in central Stockholm, working closely across functions with a shared commitment to quality, clarity, and craft.

**The role**
------------


This is a role with real impact and plenty of room to grow. You'll join the data team and work on the infrastructure that powers SCRL's analytics, forecasting, and AI-driven product features — from event ingestion all the way to the data services our apps rely on.


We're open to a range of experience levels. If you're earlier in your career, you'll learn alongside experienced data and engineering teammates and grow into broader ownership of the systems you build. If you come in with more experience, you'll have the opportunity to shape the direction of our data infrastructure and raise the bar for what we build next.


Either way, you'll work on real systems with real users — and you'll have the freedom to do the best work of your career.

**What you will do**
--------------------

**Data pipelines & infrastructure**

* Contribute to our ETL across the stack, helping make it easier to use, more reliable, and clearer when something goes wrong.
* Build and maintain ingestion pipelines from the services we rely on (RevenueCat, Firebase Analytics, attribution providers, ad networks, and others) via API and event streaming.
* Work in our BigQuery warehouse — schema design, Terraform-managed infrastructure, partitioning, and query optimization for a dataset that serves millions of users.
* Develop and maintain GCP Cloud Functions and Cloud Run services for event processing, transformations, and scheduled jobs.
* Help evolve our internal Python framework for shared models, utilities, and pipeline components used across the data codebase.

**Analytics & modeling support**

* Bring data scientists' and analysts' models into production so they can be consumed reliably by the apps, backend services, and internal tools.
* Build and maintain the data services that power product features such as template recommendations and personalization.
* Support subscription analytics infrastructure: cohorting, LTV, ROAS, retention, and forecasting pipelines across iOS and Android.
* Develop internal libraries and shared SQL/Python primitives for app metrics calculation, used by the broader team.

**Data quality, observability & craft**

* Help implement monitoring, alerting, and data quality checks so we catch issues before stakeholders do.
* Contribute to lineage and documentation that makes the warehouse navigable for everyone who uses it.
* Support GDPR-compliant data handling, including consent propagation, retention policies, and deletion across systems.
* Help maintain consistency of event schemas and metric definitions across web and mobile platforms.
* Be a contributor to — and over time a champion of — data culture across the company.

**You should have**
-------------------

**Technical foundations**

* **Languages**: Solid proficiency in Python and SQL. Comfort with TypeScript/NodeJS or similar for service work is a plus, not a requirement.
* **Warehousing**: Hands-on experience with cloud data warehouses, ideally BigQuery — or strong fundamentals you're ready to transfer.
* **Pipelines**: Some experience building or contributing to production data pipelines, from ingestion through transformation to serving.
* **Cloud infrastructure**: Exposure to GCP (or AWS/Azure) is a strong plus. Familiarity with infrastructure as code (e.g. Terraform) is welcome but can be learned on the job.

**Mindset & approach**

* **Curiosity and care for craft**: You take pride in the quality of what you ship and want to keep getting better at it.
* **Data pipelines owner mindset**: You're interested in fully managing data flows from ingestion to processing, even if you haven't done it end-to-end yet.
* **Quality and pragmatism**: You can work at a healthy pace without compromising the things that matter — clarity, reliability, and correctness.
* **Can-do attitude**: Self-motivated problem solver with an eye for detail and an internal drive for excellence.

**Product & business understanding**

* **Subscription apps**: Experience with or strong interest in subscription-based app businesses and freemium models.
* **Cross-platform thinking**: Interest in the complexities of unifying data across web, iOS, and Android.
* **Analytics-driven approach**: Comfortable working closely with analysts and data scientists, and wanting the data layer you build to support the insights the business needs.

**Collaboration & communication**

* **Team player**: You work closely and generously with analysts, data scientists, backend, and mobile engineers.
* **Clear communication**: You can explain what you're working on in a way that makes sense to teammates who don't share your technical background.

**We offer**
------------

* **High-impact role**: You'll work on the data platform that powers analytics, forecasting, and AI features for millions of users.
* **Room to grow**: Take ownership of systems from early on, with support from experienced teammates and the space to develop into broader scope over time.
* **Startup environment**: Fast-paced decision making with direct impact on product direction.
* **Strong team**: Work alongside experienced data and engineering professionals in central Stockholm.
* **Competitive package**: Market-rate salary with opportunities for professional development.
* **Creative culture**: Be part of a profitable company that values creativity and user experience.

title: Cloud Network specialist | SEB, Sweden
description:
Join our innovative team to shape the future of cloud networking within SEB’s progressive tech landscape. You’ll drive network development for our GCP and Azure environment, making a tangible impact in a collaborative, agile setting.
   

  

 SEB is more than just a bank with an IT function. We are a progressive tech company powered by innovation and fuelled by collaboration. From COBOL to AI and everything in between, we offer you an impressive tech stack and several technology paths. Together we’re innovating for a sustainable society and building the tech arena of the future, a welcoming space for everyone. Are you ready to take on a new challenge? If so, you’ll be interested in this opportunity!
   

  

 About the role:
   

  

 As a Cloud Network Specialist, you will primarily manage network related topics within our Core & Cloud team, developing and maintaining its infrastructure, including Network Security and automation. You’ll balance maintenance with new development in an agile framework, continuously striving for process improvements, standardization, and automation for speed and precision. We value your commitment to sharing knowledge, ensuring secure solutions, and minimizing overlapping functionality. You will also apply analytical thinking to identify and resolve complex technical challenges and collaborate flexibly across virtual teams.
   

  

 In this role work on-call during nights and weekends every 4-6 weeks and from time-to-time maintenance work outside office hours will be included.
   

  

 To thrive in this role, we believe you have:
   

  

* Proficiency in cloud networking across GCP and Azure environments.
* Advanced understanding of network technology disciplines including IP Routing, Switching, Routing Protocols, WAN, Firewall, and Packet Analysis.
* Strong experience with automation tools like Terraform/ARM and Ansible, plus working in Gitlab or similar.
* Knowledge in programming using Python, Bash, PowerShell or similar scripting languages.
* Excellent collaboration skills, the ability to work independently and confidently, and a commitment to analytical thinking for problem-solving.


**What we offer:**
* Agile and modern ways of working
* A flat hierarchy and openness to share ideas, opinions and points of views
* Extensive training and learning opportunities
* Hybrid work model, meaning remote work is possible up to 2 days/week
* Innovative company at the forefront of technology


**Do you want to be part of SEB?**
 It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.
   

  

 Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today via the link in this ad, but no later than 2026-05-20. If you have questions about the position, please contact Ronnie Andersson, ronnie.andersson@seb.se, note that we will not process applications via email.
   

  

 Please be aware that our final candidates undergo background checks, a process that includes for example identity control, verification of qualifications, credit checks, company engagements and history of crime. In some cases, we also apply random drug checks. During employment, employees within SEB can also expect to undergo recurring background checks.
   

  

 SEB Sweden has a redeployment responsibility, which means that this position might be covered by internal redeployment.

title: Information Technology Service Management Consultant
description:
**Role: ITSM/ ITIL Consultant** 




**Location: Stockholm, Sweden** 




**Type: Contract** 




  





**Job Description:**



* Manage multiple concurrent changes in fast-paced, high-pressure environments while ensuring minimal disruption to services.
* Own the end-to-end change lifecycle, including intake, validation, planning, scheduling, execution oversight, and post-implementation review.
* Assess and prioritize change requests based on impact, risk, urgency, and technical/business dependencies.
* Ensure all changes comply with ITIL frameworks and established organizational governance standards.
* Plan, coordinate, and facilitate Change Advisory Board (CAB) meetings, including documentation of decisions and actions.
* Apply strong knowledge of ITIL v4 and modern IT Service Management (ITSM) practices; relevant certifications are preferred.
* Utilize ServiceNow (or equivalent ITSM tools) for change management, tracking, and reporting activities.
* Demonstrate technical understanding across cloud, networking, platform, and application layers to evaluate change impact effectively.
* Engage and communicate effectively with stakeholders, including senior leadership, ensuring clear and structured updates.
* Drive continuous improvement using data-driven analysis, root cause analysis (RCA), and customer/stakeholder satisfaction metrics.

title: IT-Supporttekniker
description:
Är du redo att ta nästa steg i din karriär inom IT-Support? Tillsammans med vår kund söker vi nu engagerade och teknisk kunnig problemlösare med ett stort servicemind.
   

  

 Som en del i vår kunds Service Desk-team kommer du hantera ärenden med ansvar för både klienthantering och teknisk support. Du får möjligheten att göra en skillnad i en roll där du bidrar till att förbättra och utveckla varsamheten varje dag.
   

  

****Huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter:****
* Hantering av tekniska problem relaterade till datorer, mobiltelefoner, skrivare och annan IT-utrustning.
* Felsökning av mjuk- och hårdvara samt support för Mac och Windows.
* On- och off-boarding av medarbetare, inklusive installation och konfigurering av arbetsutrustning.
* Grundläggande systemadministration i Active Directory, hantering av gruppolicyer och dokumentation av ärenden.
* Delta i projekt för att bidra till förbättring och uppgraderingar av system.
* Underhåll av kunskapsartiklar och dokumentation för att säkerställa en användarvänlig supportupplevelse.


****Vi söker dig som har:****
* Erfarenhet av Google Workspace och ärendehanteringssystem.
* Erfarenhet av Intune och Active Directory
* Kunskaper inom datorhårdvara, operativsystem och nätverk.
* Erfarenhet av teknisk dokumentation och incidenthantering.
* Positiv inställning och ett passionerat intresse för ny teknik.
* Flytande svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift


****Om CONSULTING it:****
 När du börjar hos oss som konsult på CONSULTING it blir du en del av vår gemenskap. Du får dessutom kollegor runt om i Sverige! Du har en dedikerad konsultchef som stöttar dig och din utveckling. Vi hjälper dig hitta nya vägar genom att prova på nya roller och nya branscher, för oss är det viktigt att du trivs på din arbetsplats. Vi ser till att du hamnar på ett bra bolag under ditt uppdrag och att uppdragen ligger i linje med hur du vill utvecklas framöver. Oavsett vad det blir, står vi bakom dig hela vägen – i ditt nästa, nästa och nästa steg. Gå med i vårt team och ta del av en spännande och utvecklande arbetsmiljö!

title: Senior Director IT
description:
Lyten is leading an industrial revolution through Lyten 3D Graphene™, a breakthrough supermaterial unlocking a new generation of products — from lithium–sulfur batteries and energy storage systems to concrete admixtures, lightweight composites, and next-generation sensors that are revolutionizing industries. Together, these innovations are making a massive global improvement and driving real-world impact across energy, mobility, construction, and defense.
   

  

 At Lyten, we believe the most meaningful careers begin with purpose — and with people who want to make a difference. We’re not just developing advanced supermaterials — we’re about to change the world as we know it, reshaping how energy is stored, how products are built, and how progress is made.
   

  

 We’re entering an exciting growth phase, scaling production across the U.S. and Europe and expanding our team of engineers, scientists, and innovators.
   

  

 Apply now to join our team and be part of something bigger than yourself — where collaboration, creativity, and purpose come together to build the technologies that will define the next century.
   

  

**Why Work at Lyten**
 At Lyten, you’ll be part of a team that’s redefining what’s possible in energy, materials, and manufacturing. We’re not just imagining the future — we’re building it today with breakthrough technologies that are changing how products are made and how industries innovate.
   

  

 Our people are the heart of that mission. From world-class scientists and engineers to creative problem-solvers in operations, manufacturing, and commercialization, every member of the Lyten team plays a role in turning bold ideas into real-world impact. We believe that the best work happens when you’re doing great things in the world — with people you like. Collaboration, curiosity, and a shared sense of purpose drive everything we do.
   

  

 What You’ll Find at Lyten
   

  

* A mission that matters: Contribute directly to solving complex challenges in energy, mobility, and materials innovation.
* Cutting-edge innovation: Work on technologies at the intersection of materials science, energy storage, and advanced manufacturing that strengthen energy security and local supply chains.
* Extraordinary people: Join a team of talented, friendly, and down-to-earth innovators who support, challenge, and inspire one another every day.
* Teamwork and culture: Experience a workplace built on trust, respect, and shared success — where collaboration fuels breakthroughs and everyone’s ideas are heard.
* Global impact: Help scale new materials and energy solutions that reinforce industrial resilience across the U.S. and Europe.
* Career growth: Be part of a fast-moving company entering a commercial growth phase, with opportunities to lead, learn, and make your mark.
* Purpose-driven values: Thrive in an environment that celebrates ingenuity, optimism, and meaningful progress — together.


 Lyten offers the opportunity to do the most important work of your career — helping build the technologies that will power the next century of innovation.
   

  

 Join us, and help transform industries, communities, and the planet with friends who share your drive to make a difference.

title: Technical Manager
description:
Are you a hands‑on technical property professional who thrives on improving buildings, optimising operations, and driving smart investments? Do you want to play a key role in one of Sweden’s most successful shopping centres while working for a leading international real estate company? If so, this could be your next step.
 







  





 As Technical Manager, you will play a central role in optimising energy performance, planning long‑term technical investments, and supporting data‑driven reporting. You will analyse operational costs, collaborate closely with contractors, suppliers, and tenants, and independently manage smaller technical projects that contribute to the centre’s long‑term performance and sustainability.
 



  





**What you will be doing…** 




**Technical Operations & Maintenance** 



* Ensure seamless daily technical operations and long‑term maintenance planning.
* Ensure statutory inspections are completed, documented, and followed up on; manage property damages and corrective actions.
* Develop operation and maintenance plans together with the Senior Technical Manager and contribute to daily operational oversight.
* Support the Sr TM and manage OPEX and related CAPEX budgets, including cost control and follow‑up
* Follow up on energy consumption, including preparation of monthly reports.
* Oversee waste‑handling processes in collaboration with contractors and ensure accurate reporting.
* Drive sustainability initiatives aligned with Better Places 2030 (energy, waste, water, certifications) and work with Sr TM to plan and execute the Long Term Energy Action Plan (LTEAP).
* Ensures that technical documentation, inspection protocols and tenant‑related technical requirements are documented, updated and accessible



  





**Supplier & Contract Management** 



* Maintain close contact with external suppliers (operations, security, cleaning, waste management) to ensure service delivery meets contractual standards.
* Support the Senior Technical Manager and Facility Manager in identifying weaknesses in supplier performance and opportunities to optimise cost and daily operations.
* Manage insurance cases and ensure timely communication with relevant stakeholders.







  





**Tenant & Project Support** 



* Oversee and participate in tenant fit‑outs from a technical perspective, working closely with project managers.
* Support technical projects and gradually take ownership of long‑term project management responsibilities.
* Act as a point of contact for tenants on operational and technical matters (e.g., Täby‑mail, Connect).







  





**Financial Administration** 



* Manage invoice flows and ensure accurate coding and approval processes.
* Collaborate with Finance, Operations Management, and department heads to develop and support annual budgets and quarterly forecasts, ensuring effective management of the centre’s service charge.
* Ensure effective leadership of all departments to meet contractual obligations, deliver budgets, and maximise operational performance within the PMPS scope.
* Take responsibility for the OPEX budget, including delivery of planned actions, execution of assigned costs and spot orders, budget follow‑up, forecasting, and financial administration.



  





**Reporting & Compliance** 



* Report key performance indicators, including energy data, to the Paris headquarters.
* Ensure accurate reporting and follow‑up in systems such as Reporting21, Momentum, and Dalux.
* Administer authority inspections and prepare for internal and external reviews (e.g., Health & Safety, BREEAM)
* Responsible administrator for the work preparation portal, making sure it is used correctly, contacting contractors, and ensuring that agreed processes are followed



  





**What skills, experience, and knowledge will you need to be successful?** 



* Relevant post-secondary technical education (e.g., property management, building operations).
* Minimum 2–3 years of experience in technical property management; commercial property experience is beneficial.
* Strong administrative skills, comfortable working with digital systems.
* Experience in shopping centres, control systems, automation, or energy-saving projects is an advantage.
* Excellent command of Swedish and English, both spoken and written.







  





**Who are we?** 




 At Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield (URW), we develop and operate a portfolio of sustainable, premium, mixed-use real estate assets across Europe and the United States. Driven by our purpose to Reinvent Being Together, we are committed to accelerating urban regeneration and rethinking how we live together in cities. Today, 2,000 dedicated individuals are working together to reshape the future of real estate and shopping centers by creating innovative, engaging spaces that truly redefine community connections.
 



  





 Our flagship destinations are more than just places; they're the heartbeat of the community. We empower our employees to make a real impact through volunteering, DE&I programs, and leading ESG efforts. URW is at the forefront of experiential entertainment, constantly pushing boundaries to craft unforgettable in-person and digital experiences. By surprising and delighting our customers, we create magical moments and reinvent togetherness in every interaction.
 



  





 Within our Northern Europe Region - spanning the Netherlands, Denmark, Sweden, and the UK - we own and manage eight exceptional assets. These include Westfield Mall of the Netherlands, Stadshart Amstelveen, Westfield London, and Westfield Stratford City.
 



  





**As an international company, our primary internal language is English. Therefore, we kindly ask that you submit your application and resume in English.** 




  





**Why work for us:** 




 We put people's development and learning at the heart of what we do. We'll help you master your role, build your technical skills, and take advantage of top-notch management and leadership programs. From face-to-face workshops to our global online learning resources, we encourage you to make learning a part of your daily routine.
 



  





 We offer a fantastic bonus scheme, and many other incentives, including company health insurance is available at a reduced tariff.
 



  





 We offer an indefinite contract, including 6-month probation time. Our company culture centres on caring for our employees and supporting their success. With diverse roles, you won't be limited to one area but have the chance to grow a broad range of skills and experiences. We also have other great perks, such as Health care allowance is 4500 SEK per year and 6 weeks’ vacation!
 



  





 Want to know more about what it's like working at URW? Visit our site
 
 https://www.urw.com/join-us/life-at-urw
 




  





**What Matters to Us:** 




 At URW, our purpose is to
 **Reinvent Being Together** 
 , reimagining how people and communities connect, socialise, and experience dynamic moments in unique and meaningful ways.
 



  





 We are proud to promote equal opportunities and embrace diversity in profiles, experiences, and skills. At URW, we believe that diversity is the cornerstone of our success, and we are dedicated to fostering an environment that respects, values, celebrates, and leverages individual differences to their fullest potential.
 



  





 We evaluate each application with fairness and without discrimination based on disability, age, gender, origin, religion, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected characteristics. Our commitment is to provide equal access to our services for everyone. If you require any adjustments for your interview, please let us know in advance, so we can support you accordingly.
 



  





 As part of our commitment to maintaining a safe and trustworthy working environment, we conduct comprehensive security checks on all employees prior to their start date. These checks may include identity verification, employment history, and criminal record screening, in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.
 



  





**Join us and be part of a team that's making a real difference in your local communities!**

title: Senior Information Technology Specialist
description:
**Mandatory skills:** 



* Redcube application support
* Strong programming experience in C#, ASP.NET, and C++
* PowerShell scripting
* Hands‑on experience with JavaScript for UI components or tool enhancements
* Strong database skills in PLSQL and MS SQL Server

title: IT Digital Workplace Support Technician
description:
*Essity, a leading global hygiene and health company and the home of brands like Libero, TENA, Libresse, Tork, Sorbact and JOBST, is offering an exciting and challenging opportunity for you who are service-minded and collaborative and want to contribute with your expertise in IT Support. We are now looking for an additional colleague to join as an IT Digital Workplace Support Technician.*
 This position will be located at the Essity head office in the Waterfront Building, just next to the Stockholm Central Station. The head office includes the Executive Management and Group Staff Functions such as Finance, Legal Affairs, Strategy & Business Development, Human Resources, Communications and Global Business Support.
   

  

 We’re looking for people who embody our values, aren’t afraid to challenge, innovate, experiment and move at a fast pace. We’re always looking for ways to improve our products and ourselves. If this is you, we’d love to talk.
   

  

 Want to get a glimpse of our culture? Check out our Essence of Essity film here
   

  

**About The Role**
 As an IT Digital Workplace Support Technician, you will use your expertise to strengthen Essity’s modern workplace environment and ensure a seamless digital experience for employees. You will identify and drive improvements within your area of technical specialization, support users both regionally and globally, and collaborate closely with global IT teams to maintain reliable and secure workplace technologies. The role involves building strong relationships with users, mentoring colleagues, and ensuring that Digital Workplace services effectively enable key business processes. You will play an important role in maintaining high service levels, ensuring compliance, and supporting business continuity during critical incidents.
   

  

  

  

**What You Will Do**
* Process and take ownership of incident tickets and service requests through the IT service management platform.
* Diagnose, troubleshoot, and resolve technical issues independently to minimize disruption.
* Monitor enduser technologies to ensure optimal performance and address issues proactively.
* Install, configure, and replace IT workplace equipment such as laptops, mobile devices, peripherals, and unified communication tools.
* Support the device lifecycle, including preparing, deploying, and retiring hardware.
* Handle user provisioning and access management tasks in coordination with global IT teams.
* Administer IT assets, maintaining accurate records of hardware and software.
* Provide training and guidance to end users on Digital Workplace technologies, including Microsoft 365, Teams and OneDrive.
* Document processes, system configurations, issues, and resolutions clearly and accurately.
* Ensure internal and external audit requirements are fulfilled at the location.
* Work closely with Operational Technologies as defined by global IT standards.
* Provide extended support during critical outages to help restore operations quickly.


**Who We Believe You Are**
 To succeed in the role, it would be an advantage if you have previous experience of technical support, preferably in a global organization working across sites collaborating within own team and supporting users. You need to have sense of urgency and we expect you to be self-driven and very customer oriented with ability to manage all types of customers. You need to be able to communicate well and understand different individuals and their needs. You are a team player with ability to work under pressure but who also likes to have fun at work. Your social skills are important as you will be dealing with many customers daily. You should be fluent in English and Swedish, in writing and speech.
   

  

 Furthermore, we believe it is an advantage if you bring experience in the following:
   

  

* High-level knowledge of commonly used software, hardware and applications.
* Understanding of network and server principles, protocols.
* Ability to troubleshoot and resolve operating system related technical issues.
* Proven experience with IT Service Management tools and ticketing systems.
* Experience in managing IT assets, including hardware and software inventory, licensing, and maintenance tracking.
* Understanding of IT security principles, including firewalls, antivirus software, encryption, and secure password management.
* Familiarity with cloud platforms and collaboration tools.


**Application** 
  

  

 Interested? If you are up for the challenge and think this sounds like you, apply online today! Kindly note that due to GDPR we will not accept applications via mail.
   

  

**What We Can Offer You**
 Our purpose, Breaking Barriers to Well-being, provides meaning to everything we do. Working at Essity means a chance to improve well-being for people and opportunities to drive positive change for the society and the environment. As an employee at Essity, you will belong to an organization where you feel valued and supported to grow and challenged to generate business results in a collaborative and open atmosphere. Innovate for Good | Excel Together | Be You with Us
   

  

 Application End Date:
   

  

 21 maj 2026
   

  

 Job Requisition ID:
   

  

 Essity269649

title: Avfallstekniker – Huddinge – Heltid
description:
**Plats** 
 : Huddinge
   

  

**Omfattning** 
 : Heltid
   

  

**Arbetstider** 
 : 6-15, måndag till fredag
   

  

**Start** 
 : Så fort som möjligt
   

  

 Vill du arbeta praktiskt, bidra till en bättre miljö och vara en del av ett engagerat team? Nu söker vi en
 **avfallstekniker** 
 till en väletablerad och modern avfallsanläggning. Här får du en viktig roll i arbetet med sortering och behandling av material – med fokus på kvalitet, säkerhet och hållbarhet.
   

  

 Rekryteringen och anställningen hanteras av
 **Simplex Bemanning** 
 . Du blir anställd av Simplex och uthyrd till vår kund.
   

  

 Om tjänsten
   

  

 Som avfallstekniker arbetar du med löpande sortering och behandling av material som inkommer från företagskunder och återvinningscentraler. Du säkerställer att avfallet hanteras korrekt för materialåtervinning, energiutvinning eller deponering.
   

  

 Arbetet utförs med hjälp av maskiner såsom:
   

  

* Hjullastare
* Materialhanterare
* Truck


 Du är en viktig del i att skapa en säker, effektiv och välfungerande anläggning – både för kollegor och besökare.
   

  

 Dina huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter
   

  

* Sortera och hantera inkommande material enligt gällande rutiner
* Säkerställa ordning och en trygg arbetsmiljö på anläggningen
* Utföra dagliga kontroller och förebyggande underhåll av maskiner
* Rapportera avvikelser samt behov av service och reparation
* Kvalitetssäkra material och uppmärksamma felaktig sortering
* Bidra med förbättrings- och utvecklingsförslag inom verksamheten


 Vi söker dig som har
   

  

**Krav**
* Hjulastarutbildning för godshantering
* Truckkort A2–A4 och C2
* B-körkort
* Gymnasieutbildning eller motsvarande
* Grundläggande IT-kunskaper
* Goda kunskaper i svenska (tal och skrift)


**Meriterande**
* Erfarenhet av att köra hjulgrävare/materialhanterare
* Yrkesbevis
* Kunskaper i engelska


 Personliga egenskaper
   

  

 För att trivas i rollen ser vi att du:
   

  

* Kan arbeta i högt tempo och hantera pressade situationer
* Är flexibel och anpassningsbar
* Har mycket god samarbetsförmåga
* Har god självinsikt och kan ge och ta emot konstruktiv feedback
* Tar ansvar och arbetar strukturerat


 Om arbetsplatsen
   

  

 Du blir en del av en verksamhet med högt fokus på arbetsmiljö, säkerhet och kvalitet. Anläggningen arbetar aktivt med att utveckla moderna och effektiva processer inom avfallshantering, med målet att bidra till en mer hållbar framtid.
   

  

 Om Simplex Bemanning
   

  

 Det är
 **Simplex Bemanning** 
 som ansvarar för hela rekryteringsprocessen och anställningen. Vi arbetar nära våra kunder och matchar rätt person till rätt uppdrag. Hos oss får du en trygg anställning, tydlig kommunikation och en arbetsgivare som bryr sig om din utveckling.
   

  

 Låter detta som något för dig?
   

  

 Skicka in din ansökan redan idag – urval sker löpande!

title: Service Manager
description:
**Description:** 




  





 We are looking for a Service Manager to support delivery and coordination of SCADA services with our clientproduction environment.
 



 The role focuses on ensuring stable service delivery, supporting production needs, and coordinating activities across multiple stakeholders and teams.
 



 You will be responsible for managing service delivery in line with agreed SLAs and quality targets, while acting as a key point of contact between internal teams and stakeholders.
 



 The role also includes contributing to the ongoing evaluation and development of the Service Manager function within the organization.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities: -** 




  




* Own end-to-end delivery of SCADA-related services and ensure SLA and quality targets are met.
* Act as a point of contact between internal teams and stakeholders.
* Support requests from production on existing lines
* Drive upgrades and changes together with Production and Maintenance.
* Establish and maintain collaboration between Project Managers and stakeholders.
* Coordinate and support server-related infrastructure for production units.
* Manage service performance reviews and reporting.
* Follow up on KPIs and SLA compliance.
* Coordinate cross-functional teams to resolve service-related issues.
* Act as escalation point for incidents and service disruptions.
* Coordinate with Group Managers regarding incidents and issues.
* Support Service Managers in problem management activities.
* Review change records and approvals.
* Validate documentation and ensure service readiness.



  





**Experience & Skills: -** 




  




* Experience working with service delivery, service management, or similar roles.
* Understanding of SCADA environments or industrial IT systems.
* Experience working with incidents, problem, and change management processes.
* Ability to coordinate multiple stakeholders and cross-functional teams.
* Experience working with KPIs, SLAs, and service follow-up.
* General understanding of IT infrastructure, including servers.

title: Technical Support Manager
description:
**We are hiring a Specialist Technical Support to join our team.**
**We are looking for you on a fixed-term contract as parental leave cover.**
**Let’s talk about the role:**
 The Technical Support Manager Sweden reports to the Nordics Technical Service Manager and is a key member of the Technical Services Department. The role is based on-site at the ResMed Swedish office in Kista and will support customers, internal teams, and distributors within the Swedish market.
   

  

**Position Description:**
 The primary purpose of this role is to provide high-quality technical and engineering support for ResMed products and 3pp products, both to customers and internal stakeholders. This includes ownership of customer queries, management of investigation of product failures, device upgrades, maintenance, testing, technical trainings and management of repairs. The Technical Services Manager also acts as the local QA/RA representative, ensuring regulatory compliance and device traceability throughout the product life cycle. Beyond the day-to-day operational responsibilities, this position plays a key role in representing the Swedish Technical Services function within
 **local, regional, and global meetings and projects** 
 . The successful candidate will actively participate in strategic and operational discussions, ensuring alignment with ResMed’s global service structure, processes, and long-term objectives. This includes contributing to continuous improvement initiatives, sharing local market insights, and supporting the implementation of new global service strategies in the Nordic region.
   

  

**Let’s talk about responsibilities:**
* Provide technical support to local customers via phone, email, and CRM tools (mandatory for tender participation).
* Manage troubleshooting, repair, returns, and replacement processes for sleep apnea devices, life support ventilators, and diagnostic devices, including non-serialized product handling.
* Deliver technical support to internal teams including sales, finance, and customer service.
* Act as the Local QA/RA SCHU representative, responsible for complaint handling, device traceability, and liaison with regulatory authorities and RCHU.
* Provide technical support to distributors in Sweden.
* Manage the aftersales online portal: create customer accounts and conduct user training sessions for hospital technical teams and distributors.
* Conduct technical training and manage re-certification processes for hospital staff (remote or on-site as required by tenders).
* Prepare and deliver technical documentation for tenders, including service manuals, quality certificates, and official tender forms in the local language.
* Manage loan devices, including shipment, return, and record-keeping.
* Drive continuous improvement initiatives: report KPIs, update OKRs, improve Net Promoter Score (NPS), and communicate customer needs to the sales team.
* Coordinate logistics and customs clearance paperwork for service devices.
* Perform other duties and responsibilities as assigned.
* Participate in regional, European and global meetings with the global technical teams.


**Let’s talk about qualifications and experience:**
**Required:**
* University degree in applied sciences,engineering or biomedical science.
* Native Swedish language skills and business-level English.
* Knowledge of measurement units and electronic components.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) and CRM tools.
* Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to maintain professionalism and interact effectively with customers and stakeholders at all levels.
* Ability to engage with senior internal and external personnel and drive resolutions that align with company goals and objectives.


**Preferred:**
* Experience working within the hospital or healthcare sector.
* Minimum 2 years’ experience in a similar technical service role using a CRM system (e.g., Salesforce).
* Knowledge of the Swedish healthcare system and/or experience managing relationships with healthcare professionals.
* Hands-on experience in technical repair and/or daily service center operations.
* Familiarity with 5S, Lean, or Six Sigma methodologies.
* Knowledge of an additional Nordic language (Finnish, Norwegian, Danish).
* Proactive and self-driven
* Customer- and business-oriented mindset
* Comfortable with digital communication tools (phone, Zoom, Teams).
* Highly organized and detail oriented.
* Eager to challenge themselves and grow.
* Strong team player with a collaborative approach.


**Additional information :**
* At ResMed, all employees benefit from a bonus plan, the percentage of which depends on your position within the organisation
* You also have access to a referral bonus and to ResMed's preferred shareholding programme
* Internal career opportunity - joining an international fast-pace and massively growing company


**OK, so what's next?**
 Joining us is more than saying “yes” to making the world a healthier place. It’s discovering a career that’s challenging, supportive and inspiring. Where a culture driven by excellence helps you not only meet your goals, but also create new ones. We focus on creating a diverse and inclusive culture, encouraging individual expression in the workplace and thrive on the innovative ideas this generates. If this sounds like the workplace for you, apply now.

Where a culture driven by excellence helps you not only meet your goals, but also create new ones. We focus on creating a diverse and inclusive culture, encouraging individual expression in the workplace and thrive on the innovative ideas this generates.
