title: Content Specialist
description:
Datasite and its associated businesses are the global center for facilitating economic value creation for companies across the globe. From data rooms to AI deal sourcing
   

  

 and more. Here you’ll find the finest technological pioneers: Datasite, Blueflame AI, Grata, and Sherpany. They all, collectively, define the future for business growth.
   

  

 Apply for one position or as many as you like. Talent doesn’t always just go in one direction or fit in a single box. We’re happy to see whatever your superpower is and find the best place for it to flourish.
   

  

 Get started now, we look forward to meeting you.
   

  

**Job Description**
 Valu8 for Datasite is looking for a high-output Content Specialist who can turn complex topics into clear, compelling content for a demanding B2B audience. This role sits at the core of Valu8’s go-to-market efforts and supports campaigns, events, webinars, sales enablement, and thought leadership.
   

  

 The role is hands-on and execution-focused. You will own content delivery end to end and collaborate closely with demand generation, sales, and product to ensure Valu8’s expertise is consistently reflected in everything we publish.
   

  

 This role is based at Valu8’s office in Stockholm.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities
   

  

* Produce written content for Valu8’s ICP, including articles, LinkedIn posts, newsletters, emails, scripts, and case studies
* Develop content for campaigns, events, webinars, and sales enablement
* Support Valu8-hosted events by identifying and coordinating relevant speakers and panel participants, and shaping agendas and discussion themes
* Support production of webinars, interviews, and video content
* Repurpose and adapt content across channels such as email, social media, landing pages, and ads
* Collaborate closely with sales and product to extract insights, stories, and proof points
* Build and maintain a content calendar to ensure consistent, high-quality delivery
* Uphold clarity, quality, and substance across all content outputs


 Ideal Profile
   

  

* 1–4 years of experience in content marketing, journalism, communications, or a similar field
* Strong writing skills with the ability to explain complex topics clearly and precisely
* Experience creating B2B content; background in finance, SaaS, consulting, or data is a strong plus
* Comfortable contributing to webinars or video content; production or editing experience is a bonus
* Familiar with tools such as Figma and Canva
* Highly organised, reliable, and structured in execution
* Proactive, detail-oriented, and quality-driven
* Interest in finance, private markets, or data-driven products is a plus


 Our company is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workforce where all individuals are respected and valued. We are an equal opportunity employer and make all employment decisions without regard to race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and are dedicated to building teams that reflect the diversity of our communities.

title: AI QA Engineer
description:
We are looking for an
 **AI QA Engineer** 
 with hands-on experience using AI tools in real software testing workflows.
   

  

 This is not a traditional manual QA role, and it is not for someone who is only curious about AI. We are looking for someone who has already used tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, or similar to improve test planning, test automation, risk analysis, bug reporting, and release quality.
   

  

 You will work across multiple projects in parallel and help our teams test smarter, move faster, and catch issues earlier.
   

  

**The role**
 You will help us improve quality across our product areas by combining structured QA, automation, and AI-assisted workflows.
   

  

 Your work will include system testing, end-to-end testing, regression planning, test automation, reviewing requirements and changes, identifying edge cases, analyzing bugs and logs, and helping teams understand release risks before they reach production.
   

  

 A key part of the role is using AI practically to increase test coverage, generate and improve test cases, support automation, summarize changes, analyze issues, and create repeatable QA processes that can scale across several teams.
   

  

**We Are Looking For Someone Who Has**
* Experience as a QA Engineer, Test Engineer, or Test Automation Engineer
* Hands-on experience using AI tools as part of real QA work
* Strong understanding of system testing and end-to-end testing
* Experience with test automation tools such as Playwright, Cypress, Selenium, Postman, or similar
* Ability to work across several projects in parallel
* Good technical understanding and ability to collaborate closely with developers
* A structured way of working and strong attention to detail


**It is a plus if you have**
* Experience testing web applications, APIs, integrations, payments, SaaS, POS, or e-commerce systems
* Experience with JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, or CI/CD pipelines
* Experience reviewing pull requests or reading code
* Experience building AI-assisted QA workflows, prompts, or test automation processes


**Who You Are**
 You are a modern QA professional who already uses AI to work smarter.
   

  

 You understand that AI can help speed up testing, but you also know that quality still depends on human judgment, product understanding, and structured thinking.
   

  

 You are comfortable switching between projects, quickly understanding context, spotting risks, and helping teams release with more confidence.
   

  

**About Us**
 We build digital systems used by restaurants and food businesses every day, including POS, online ordering, self-service kiosks, payments, integrations, and customer-facing tools.
   

  

 Because our systems are used in real operational environments, quality and stability are critical. We want to use AI and automation to make our QA process smarter, faster, and more scalable.

title: Senior Operations Specialist, Supply Risks & Compliance
description:
**About Wolt**
 At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
   

  

 Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
   

  

 Regulations across our markets are evolving fast - from food safety rules to language requirements, vehicle registrations, contract model changes and more. Every new requirement impacts courier supply: conversion rates, time-to-convert, churn, and forecasting becomes harder.
   

  

 As our
 **Supply Risks & Compliance Senior Specialist** 
 , you will connect the dots between regulatory changes and our courier supply reality. You’ll partner closely with the teams implementing new compliance requirements, assess risk early, translate impact into clear assumptions, and help our Supply teams prepare for what’s coming. Your work will keep our growth predictable, our teams aligned, and our supply resilient.
   

  

**What You’ll Be Doing**
* Monitor compliance and regulatory changes across North region markets and assess their impact on courier supply, including acquisition, conversion and activation.
* Quantify supply risks: conversion shifts, time-to-convert, CAC implications, pool health, churn patterns, and forecasting uncertainty.
* Partner with cross-functional teams (Legal, Compliance, Operations, Marketing, Finance), as well as external partners such as temporary agencies in Sweden, to ensure supply considerations shape implementation plans.
* Facilitate timely escalations to markets and senior stakeholders, with clear risk framing and recommended actions.
* Build assumptions for impact on KPIs, budgets, workload, and acquisition plans; align with Supply teams on timelines and mitigation strategies.
* Prepare structured briefs and playbooks that translate regulatory changes into clear operational steps for Supply.
* Track the execution of compliance-driven changes and evaluate their real-world impact on supply predictability and performance.


**Our humble expectations**
**Must-haves**
* 3-5 years of experience in Operations, Growth, or Supply roles where change or regulatory shifts influenced performance.
* Strong analytical skills - able to interpret performance metrics, quantify impact, and support short- to mid-term forecasting.
* Experience building simple operational models or forecasting frameworks that translate regulatory or funnel changes into expected business outcomes.
* Ability to connect technical/legal changes to operational consequences for funnel health, churn, or forecasting accuracy.
* Confident communicator who can align stakeholders across functions (Legal, Compliance, Ops, Marketing).
* Experience preparing structured recommendations, risk assessments, or impact briefs for senior stakeholders.
* Comfortable working with ambiguity: can bring structure to unclear tasks, work with incomplete information, and adapt as requirements evolve.
* Familiar with modern tools, including the practical use of AI or GPT-based assistants to improve speed, structuring, or analysis.
* Fluent in English (written and spoken), and full professional proficiency in Swedish.
* Ability to work from our Stockholm office three days a week with two optional home office days.


**Nice to have**
* Exposure to multi-country operations or markets with frequent regulatory change.
* Familiarity with courier onboarding or supply acquisition funnels.
* Experience working with dashboards or data tools (Looker, Tableau, Data Studio, SQL basics).
* Background collaborating with Legal, Compliance, or Policy teams.


**Next steps**
 If this sounds like the right challenge, we’d love to meet you. Apply now, and
 **please submit your application and resume in English** 
 !
   

  

 The process includes conversations with the talent acquisition partner, hiring managers and a practical assignment focused on structuring risk and impact, and a final cross-functional discussion.
   

  

 Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
   

  

 We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

title: Account Manager
description:
**About Kaseya**
 Kaseya is the leading provider of AI-powered IT management and cybersecurity software, serving Managed Service Providers (MSPs) and internal IT organizations worldwide. Our comprehensive platform helps organizations efficiently manage, secure, and automate their IT environments, driving operational efficiency and long-term business success.
   

  

 Backed by Insight Partners, a leading global software investor, Kaseya has experienced sustained double-digit growth and continues to expand its global footprint. Today, Kaseya supports customers in more than 20 countries and manages over 15 million endpoints worldwide.
   

  

 Founded in 2000, Kaseya has built a culture centered around innovation, accountability, and results. We are a high-growth, high-performance organization that values individuals who are driven, adaptable, and committed to delivering exceptional outcomes for our customers and teammates alike.
   

  

 At Kaseya, success comes from embracing challenges, moving with urgency, and continuously raising the bar.
   

  

**Position Summary**
 Kaseya is looking for an enthusiastic and results-driven
 **Account Manager** 
 to focus on the expansion of existing accounts. In this role, you will be responsible for identifying new growth opportunities within your portfolio, strengthening relationships with existing clients, and driving revenue growth through strategic account management.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Account Expansion: Proactively engage with current customers to identify upsell and cross-sell opportunities, leveraging Kaseya’s full suite of products to drive revenue growth.
* Customer Relationship Management: Build and maintain strong relationships with key decision-makers, ensuring customer satisfaction while aligning their needs with Kaseya’s solutions.
* Strategic Sales Execution: Develop tailored strategies for each account, including regular check-ins and business reviews, to identify new opportunities and overcome potential challenges.
* Manage Adoption Metrics: Monitor customer usage, proactively addressing adoption challenges and following established sales processes to drive cross-sell, upsell, and expansion strategies.
* Revenue Growth: Take ownership of your sales targets, consistently exceeding quotas while delivering exceptional value to clients. Actively contribute to both short-term and long-term revenue generation strategies.
* Collaboration: Work closely with internal teams, including Customer Success and Sales, to deliver exceptional customer experiences and ensure smooth implementation of new solutions.
* CRM Management: Utilize Salesforce to manage leads, opportunities, and accounts, ensuring all interactions are logged and tracked to drive sales success.


**Required Qualifications**
* 2+ years’ experience in Saas sales (preferably in the Swedish market)
* Driven, passionate about sales, and eager to earn significant sales commissions
* Exceptional inside sales experience in a metrics driven, fast-paced sales environment
* Has a proven track record of crushing sales goals
* A motivated, coachable and competitive go-getter, a resilient achiever driven to win and excel
* An energetic team player with exceptional organizational, interpersonal, and communication skills
* Create weekly, monthly and rolling 6 weeks’ sales forecasts.
* Exposure to CRM applications (Salesforce) to successfully manage leads, opportunities and accounts


**Additional Information**
*Kaseya provides equal employment opportunity to all employees and applicants without regard to race, religion, age, ancestry, gender, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, veteran status, marital status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.*

title: Account Specialist - Retail
description:
**About Wolt**
 At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
   

  

 Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
   

  

 At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 1000 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
   

  

 Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
   

  

**About The Role**
 At Wolt, we’re looking for an Account Specialist to join our Retail team - an exciting opportunity for someone with a few years of experience who’s ready to take ownership of their own portfolio and drive partner performance.
   

  

 In this role, you’ll independently manage and develop a portfolio of retail partners, focusing on improving performance, identifying growth opportunities, and building strong day-to-day relationships. You’ll use data to guide your decisions and play a key role in delivering results for both our partners and Wolt.
   

  

 If you enjoy working hands-on with partners, taking initiative, and turning insights into action, this could be the role for you.
   

  

 What you’ll be doing
   

  

**Day-to-day in this role you’ll:**
* Own and manage a portfolio of small and medium-sized retail partners on a day-to-day basis
* Monitor partner performance and take action to improve key metrics
* Prepare and lead regular check-ins with partners, sharing insights and actionable recommendations
* Identify opportunities to grow partner performance and execute on agreed actions
* Support partners in using Wolt tools such as campaigns, promotions, and ads effectively
* Work cross-functionally with Marketing, Operations, and Support to solve partner needs
* Plan and execute campaigns together with partners to drive orders and engagement
* Use data to track results and adjust your approach based on what works
* Proactively suggest ways to improve partner visibility and performance on the platform
* Contribute to team initiatives and support less experienced team members when relevant


 Our humble expectations
   

  

* 1–3 years of experience in account management, sales, or another partner-facing role in a fast-paced environment
* Fluent in Swedish and English, with the ability to communicate clearly and adapt your message to different audiences
* Comfortable working independently, while knowing when to seek guidance or escalate when needed
* Strong relationship-building skills, with a natural ability to connect with partners and colleagues
* Analytical mindset, with the ability to turn data into simple, actionable insights
* Structured and detail-oriented, with strong follow-through
* Proactive and hands-on - you take initiative and get things done
* Collaborative and team-oriented, with a willingness to share knowledge and support others
* Experience in retail, e-commerce, or FMCG is a plus


 What we offer
   

  

 This is a great opportunity to take the next step in your account management career, with more ownership and responsibility for your own portfolio.
   

  

 You’ll work in a collaborative and fast-paced environment, where you’ll continue to develop your commercial and analytical skills, while gaining experience that prepares you for more senior roles over time.
   

  

 Psst…We also offer breakfast every monday, “Friday Fika” and plenty of after work’s to socialise with your new colleagues!
   

  

 Why you’ll love it
   

  

* 🚀 A fast-growing company: Be part of an ambitious, international team
* 🤩 A strong learning environment: Develop your commercial and relationship-building skills from day one
* 💪 Real impact: Take ownership and see the results of your work
* 💙 Great team culture: Work with smart, friendly, and supportive colleagues


 Next steps
   

  

 If you are excited about working in a high-growth environment, taking ownership, and being part of an extremely ambitious team, then click below to apply, make sure to add your CV and get the conversation going!
   

  

 We will be reviewing applications on an on-going basis. If this sounds like an opportunity you want to pursue, apply today! 💙
   

  

 Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
   

  

 We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

title: Category Manager
description:
foodora is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in over 70+ countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.
   

  

**Job Description**
 Do you want to be the superhero of Sweden's grocery retail revolution? Do you have a black (or pink) belt in category management and grocery sales? If you're nodding your head in excitement, keep reading! We're on the hunt for a category mastermind to join our team and help us unleash the awesomeness of Sweden's trendiest digital food chain. At foodora market, we're not just breaking records, we're breaking the mold for the entire grocery industry and now it’s your time to join the ride!
   

  

* Acting as the “business owner” and be fully responsible for the P&L performance of the assigned category
* End to end category management including managing performance of the product portfolio, developing and growing supplier relationships, expanding product selection and supplier base, leading negotiations, setting category strategy, driving sales and margin improvements and creating best-in-class customer experiences
* Ensure foodora market categories has the optimal selection and strategically competitive prices in the market through local and international sourcing
* Source and build strong supplier base to negotiate terms improvement, secure supplier advertising spend, and ensure a reliable and operationally effective flow of goods
* Work to develop new sourcing and extended range to ensure we have high quality, good value goods available to our customers year-round
* Employ data-driven decision making to drive the category. Provide insight to the commercial team and work closely with marketing and supply chain to forecast demand and ensure consistent supply
* Partner with supply chain team to manage inventory levels and wastage
* Work cross functionally with Product, Supply Chain, Operations and Marketing to deliver strategic projects


**Qualifications**
* 3-5 years experience in merchandising / buying, retail and category management
* Experience in supplier negotiations, pricing and promotion, inventory management and product development
* Superb business judgment with a track record of successful negotiations and overall relationship management (both internal and external)
* Ability to work autonomously in a highly demanding and often ambiguous environment, with strong attention to detail and exceptional organizational skills
* Strong analytical skills
* Great communication skills (both oral and written) are a must
* Mastery of MS Office (particularly Excel, Access, and PowerPoint) is preferred
* A plus is if you are highly analytical and strong in following up sales performance, margins, and key commercial KPIs.
* Experience from roles such as controller, strategic purchaser, or similar analytical positions within purchasing or retail is considered a strong advantage, as it brings a structured, data-driven approach to category performance and decision-making.


 Studies have shown that women and some minorities are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every qualification. If you are interested in this role and you have most of the skills we’re looking for, we encourage you to apply anyway!
   

  

**Additional Information**
* Wellness allowance topped with the opportunity to participate in many work/life balance initiatives at foodora such as free group training at Sats & reduced price on massage.
* Employee discount at foodora (woho!) and awesome Friday breakfasts!
* Occupational pension, incl. premium exemption insurance, accident insurance and life insurance.
* foodora Learning - Monthly learning sessions about different topics, such as the q-commerce industry, negotiation techniques and project management combined with a structured onboarding and inspiring courses within our learning tool Sana
* Great deals at Benify (for e.g. gym membership, e-bikes hotels, audiobooks, streaming services, clothes, kitchen supplies… and so much more!
* Awesome AW’s and (pink) parties!

title: Technology Strategy Consultant
description:
*Join Accenture Technology Strategy team at our Stockholm office and advise our clients through their digital transformation journeys.*
 We offer you the opportunity to join and shape our journey as we work with some of the largest and most innovative companies in the world. In this role you will provide expertise and insights for Accenture clients through value-driven recommendations in the intersection between business and technology strategy.
   

  

**About Accenture** 
**Technology Strategy & Transformation practice:**
 We help our clients become industry leaders by combining strategy, technology, and consulting expertise. Working closely with our clients, we identify their key focus areas in the market and define the organizational and digital capabilities needed to reach their target state. Our work enables sustainable, technology‑driven transformation, creating value across financial performance, customer and employee experience, sustainability, talent, and inclusion & diversity.
   

  

 Below are examples with areas you could work with:
   

  

  

- Advise clients on how to develop their business by leveraging emerging and established technologies, identifying client challenges and shaping strategic recommendations

- Create intelligent operating models and strategy roadmaps to transform and build organizations ready for growth and stability, from strategy through implementation

- Unlock the value of data, architecture, and AI to drive business agility and transformation to a real-time enterprise

- Identify, quantify, maximize, and sustain value realization from technology investments

- And much more!

 We pride ourselves in building long-lasting relationships with our clients by driving powerful outcomes and inspiring with impact.
   

  

**Who we are looking for:**
 We are looking for curious people willing to question the current ways of working by utilizing new technologies, agile ways of working and new business models to bring new ideas and innovations to life. With us you will have the opportunity to work with large scale business transformations enabled by digital and be a key advisor for c-suites in leading Nordic companies.
   

  

 We hope you have the following:
   

  

  

- Master's degree in a relevant field with strong academic performance

- 3+ years of relevant experience from top tier consulting and/or working with strategy, operating models, future tech, cloud strategy, tech transformation etc.

- An analytical and problem-solving mindset, who can interpret complex data, achieve impressive results and thrive in settings where you define, structure, and solve clients pain points

- Ability to identify and pursue new business opportunities

- Put ideas and findings into the right context for the right audience, both written and verbally in English and preferably in Swedish

- In addition to delivering strategy projects, we are looking for experience in team-leading and driving internal activities (e.g. recruiting, social, training, etc.).

- Flexibility in terms of travel and work across the Nordics is expected

**What we can offer you:**
- Competitive benefit package: truly competitive salary terms, bonus schemes, pension, health insurance, employee stock purchase program and other benefits.

- Market-leading expertise and great learning opportunities: we use our global network of experts to stay up to date on technology and industry trends to be relevant in client dialogue - and we are never fully trained.

- Work in a team dedicated to complex problem solving: utilize the sheer variety of work we do, and the experience it offers, which provide an unbeatable platform from which to advance your career.

- Fun at work and work-life balance - we make good arrangements to combine family life (and leisure time in general) with work life.

**Are you excited by the opportunity?** 
 Apply now and attach your CV, Cover Letter and university grades in English. We look forward to receiving your application!

title: Data Modeller – One Data Ecosystem (ECP)
description:
**Role: Data Modeller – One Data Ecosystem (ECP)** 




**Location: Stockholm, Sweden** 




**Type: Contract** 




**Mode: Hybrid (2-3days/week)** 




  





**Job Description:** 




**Key Responsibilities** 




 • Design and deliver L2 (Logical) and L3 (Physical) data models in line with layered data architecture standards
 



 • Design data products optimized for modern cloud-based data platforms
 



 • Ensure scalability, maintainability, and reusability of data models
 



 • Produce clear and comprehensive documentation for all delivered data models
 



 • Define and propose a governance process enabling continuous maintenance and development
 



 • Communicate data modelling concepts and outcomes to internal stakeholders
 



 • Ensure effective knowledge transfer to resources
 



**Required Technical Skills** 




**Data Modelling** 




 • Conceptual, logical (L2), and physical (L3) data modelling
 



 • Dimensional modelling (star and snowflake schemas)
 



 • Normalized and denormalized data structures
 



 • Data product–oriented modelling approaches
 



 • Ability to translate business requirements into technical data models
 



**Cloud Data Architecture & Platforms** 




 • Designing data models for modern cloud-based data platforms
 



 • Hands-on exposure to Azure Data Lake (ADLS)
 



 • Experience working with Databricks and lakehouse architectures
 



 • Strong understanding of layered data architectures (raw, curated, consumption)
 



 • Awareness of performance, scalability, and cost considerations in cloud analytics platforms
 



**Metadata, Documentation & Governance** 




 • Metadata modelling and structured documentation
 



 • Data lineage and traceability concepts
 



 • Data governance principles including ownership and stewardship
 



 • Defining sustainable processes for long-term model maintenance
 



**Tools & Communication** 




 • Experience with enterprise data modelling and diagramming tools
 



 • Strong SQL knowledge to validate and reason about data models
 



 • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
 



 • Ability to explain complex data models to non-technical stakeholders

title: Security Engineer
description:
At Viaplay Group, we entertain millions of people every day through our streaming services, radio networks, and TV channels. We believe in the power of content not just as a way of telling stories and touching lives, but also expanding worlds.
   

  

 We’re looking for the best people to join us on our journey. Right now, we’re searching for a
 **Security Engineer** 
 in our
 **Stockholm** 
 office – are you ready to hit play on an exciting career change?
   

  

**The Role**
 We are seeking an experienced Security Engineer to join our IT Workplace team, working as a core team member with shared responsibility and close, hands‑on collaboration across all areas of IT. You will support security operations, governance, and compliance within an outsourced, Microsoft‑based hybrid IT environment.
   

  

 The role is responsible for protecting the organisation against cyber threats and embedding security best practices across the Group. You will work closely with colleagues and an outsourced IT partner to deliver effective and pragmatic security outcomes.
   

  

 You will act as a technical lead for security operations, incident response, and security tooling, working collaboratively rather than in isolation, and ensuring security is integrated into day‑to‑day IT operations and change initiatives.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Act as a security lead for IT modernisation and improvement initiatives
* Work with the SOC partner to monitor, investigate, and respond to security alerts and incidents across cloud, on‑premises, and SaaS environments
* Act as the technical escalation point for high‑priority security incidents, coordinating containment, remediation, and recovery activities
* Perform root cause analysis and post‑incident reviews, communicating risks and outcomes to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders.
* Administer and operate security tooling, primarily Microsoft Purview, Defender, and Entra
* Design and manage the implementation of Zero Trust controls utilising Conditional Access, PIM, RBAC, and identity governance
* Maintain and improve security policies, ensuring alignment with recognised standards and regulatory requirements such as ISO 27001 and NIS2
* Support vulnerability management activities, audits, penetration testing, and third‑party risk assessments
* Produce incident, threat, and compliance reporting for stakeholders
* Drive continuous improvement of security controls, tooling, and processes


**What We’re Looking For**
 We understand you may feel confident ticking certain boxes more than others and that’s why we always keep an open mind in our recruitment process. But, in order to thrive in this role, we do believe you’ll have at least some experience in the following:
   

  

* 5+ years’ experience in cybersecurity or security operations roles
* Hands‑on experience with Microsoft Purview, Defender, Entra, and vulnerability and threat management tools.
* Experience working with outsourced IT services and SOC partners
* Microsoft security certifications and other industry‑recognised cybersecurity certifications are highly desirable
* Strong analytical, problem‑solving, and communication skills, with the ability to produce clear technical and non‑technical documentation
* Relevant education in IT, cybersecurity, or a related discipline, or equivalent professional experience


 What we offer you
   

  

* We’ve got you covered! 30 days of paid vacation every year, an attractive pension and insurance scheme, and generous parental leave pay lift.
* Your wellbeing matters. We provide you with various wellbeing initiatives including wellness allowance.
* A safe space to grow and up-skill. Our learning culture puts you in the driver’s seat of your own development.
* An innovative environment with Hack Days once a year. This week-long initiative allows you to think outside the box and deliver creative, technical solutions that (more often than not) go on to be implemented, either in our product or our ways of working.
* Entertainment is what we love, and entertainment is what we do. So, unlimited access to Viaplay seems only fair for you to get to know the product – including serier & viewing events, new release movie rentals, linear channels and more.


**Hit play today**
 If this feels like your kind of challenge, make sure you apply by attaching your CV here – you may also want to add your LinkedIn profile. Please don’t send us your application via email because we won’t be able to accept it. We do, however, welcome any questions you may have about this particular position.
   

  

 Want to learn more about who we are and what we do? Check out our careers page or follow us on Instagram! We’re only ever a few clicks away.

title: Library Technician
description:
**Role Description/Criteria** 
 Are you passionate about connecting people with knowledge and resources? Do you combine a love of literature with technical expertise and a keen eye for detail? Join us as a Library Technician at our St Catherine Campus (Mickleham) and help our Resource Centre thrive.At Kolbe Catholic College, we are seeking a skilled professional who excels at developing dynamic collections, managing digital and print resources, and supporting the learning journeys of our community. If you can thoughtfully select engaging materials, master digital databases, and make cataloguing seamless, you could be the perfect fit for our team.
 **About the role** 
 The Library Technician will:
   

  

* Select, manage, and organise of library resources, including digital subscriptions.
* Supporting students and staff with access to resources and library software.
* Promote reading and library-based activities and clubs.
* Assist with budgeting, purchase orders, and general library administration.
* Supervise and lead student activities before and after school and during breaks.
* Other duties as required in line with operational needs and the employee’s skills.


 This is a part-time
 **(0.6 FTE)** 
 , ongoing position commencing as soon as possible, primarily based at St Catherine Campus, with potential duties at other campuses as required.
 **Skills and experience** 
 The successful candidate will demonstrate:
   

  

* Relevant qualifications and/or experience in library support roles, ideally within education.
* Strong ICT, organisational, planning, and documentation skills.
* Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to work independently and collaboratively.
* Demonstrated ability to manage competing priorities and meet deadlines.
* Flexibility, discretion, and commitment to confidentiality and Catholic values.
* A positive, enthusiastic approach to supporting student learning and school community engagement.
* Current Working with Children Check and National Police Check.


**How to apply** 
 You will need a cover letter and resume to upload with your application.
 **Applications close:** 
 Thursday, 28 May 2026, 4:00pm.Shortlisted candidate maybe interviewed before the closing dateFor more information about Kolbe Catholic College, please go to our website www.kolbecc.catholic.edu.auFor more information about our privacy collection notice please visit this Link.Kolbe Catholic College is committed to the safety, wellbeing and protection of all children in our care, including maintaining compliance with the Child Safety Code of Conduct.
   

  

* Position Description


**Application Procedures** 
 https://form.myrecruitmentplus.com/applicationform?jobAdId=9742715

title: Spontanansökan – IT-infrastrukturkonsult till Nexer Infrastructure
description:
Detta gäller inte ett specifikt uppdrag, utan framtida möjligheter vid rätt matchning.
   

  

 Är du nyfiken på nästa steg inom IT-infrastruktur?
   

  

 Vi på Nexer Infrastructure söker löpande kontakt med skickliga och engagerade personer inom IT-infrastruktur – även om vi just nu inte har ett aktivt uppdrag att tillsätta. Vi vet ännu inte exakt vilka uppdrag som kommer in i höst – men genom att skicka in en spontanansökan finns du med i våra tankar när vi söker rätt kompetens för framtida uppdrag. Så kikar du runt efter nya utmaningar och är intresserad av Nexer - skicka gärna in din ansökan!
   

  

 Vi är särskilt intresserade av dig som har erfarenhet inom något/några av områdena nedan:
   

  

* Drift, övervakning och förvaltning av IT-miljöer
* Nätverk, brandväggar och säkerhetslösningar
* Linux (Automatisering i Ansible eller Bash)
* Microsoftmiljöer (AD, Exchange, M365, Windows Server)
* Virtualisering (VMware, Hyper-V)
* Molntjänster (Azure, AWS, Google Cloud)
* ITIL och arbete med ärendehanteringssystem


 Vem är du?
   

  

 Du har flera års erfarenhet inom IT-infrastruktur och är van att arbeta både självständigt och i team. Du har ett konsultmässigt förhållningssätt, kommunicerar tydligt och gillar problemlösning.
   

  

 Vi ser gärna att du har:
   

  

* Förmåga att snabbt sätta dig in i nya miljöer
* God problemlösningsförmåga och vana att arbeta proaktivt
* Obehindrade kunskaper i svenska, både i tal och skrift


 Vad kan vi erbjuda?
   

  

 Hos oss blir du en del av ett bolag med passion, framtidstro och teknik i fokus. När rätt uppdrag dyker upp matchar vi dig med det – och stöttar dig hela vägen, både inför och under uppdraget.
   

  

 Du får ett starkt nätverk av kollegor, en engagerad konsultchef och chansen att göra skillnad ute hos våra kunder.
   

  

 Låter det intressant?
   

  

 Skicka in ditt CV och några rader om din kompetens, tillgänglighet och vilken typ av uppdrag du är intresserad av. Vi återkommer efter semestrarna om vi får in ett uppdrag som matchar din profil.

title: Solutions Consultant
description:
**Our Mission**
 At Palo Alto Networks®, we’re united by a shared mission—to protect our digital way of life. We thrive at the intersection of innovation and impact, solving real-world problems with cutting-edge technology and bold thinking. Here, everyone has a voice, and every idea counts. If you’re ready to do the most meaningful work of your career alongside people who are just as passionate as you are, you’re in the right place.
   

  

**Who We Are**
 In order to be the cybersecurity partner of choice, we must trailblaze the path and shape the future of our industry. This is something our employees work at each day and is defined by our values: Disruption, Collaboration, Execution, Integrity, and Inclusion. We weave AI into the fabric of everything we do and use it to augment the impact every individual can have. If you are passionate about solving real-world problems and ideating beside the best and the brightest, we invite you to join us!
   

  

 This role is remote, but distance is no barrier to impact. Our hybrid teams collaborate across geographies to solve big problems, stay close to our customers, and grow together. You will be part of a culture that values trust, accountability, and shared success where your work truly matters.
   

  

**Job Summary**
 The Team Our Solutions Consultant team members work hand-in-hand with large organizations around the world to keep their digital environments protected. We educate, inspire, and empower our potential clients in their journey to transform their security architectures. You are empowered with unmatched systems and tools and a team built on joint success. You won’t find someone at Palo Alto Networks that isn’t committed to your success – with everyone pitching in to assist when it comes to solutions selling, learning, and development. Our Solutions Consulting community is driven by the mission to be our customers’ cybersecurity partner of choice, protecting their digital way of life. Job Summary The Solutions Consultant is the evolution of the traditional Sales Engineering role, aligning how we best serve our customers in understanding their environment, providing solution guidance and ensuring value realization in their investment with Palo Alto Networks. As a Solutions Consultant you provide technical leadership and expertise and guidance in your customer’s security transformation journey. You will play a key role in defining technical solutions that secure a customer’s key business imperatives and ensuring value realization of their investment with Palo Alto Networks. You evangelize our industry leadership in on-prem, cloud, and security operations services that establish PANW as your customer’s cybersecurity partner of choice. Key Responsibilities Curiosity is core to the Solutions Consultant role, and you see complex problems as opportunities to learn and deliver innovative solutions! You define your impact by: Meeting and exceeding sales quotas by building and implementing strategic, technical account plans that target cross-platform solutions Understands Key customer business requirements and has the ability to position, demonstrate and create high level designs across the entire PANW portfolio solutions creating business value for customers Ability to drive customer adoption of Palo Alto Networks Platform. Building customer relationships by helping customers achieve increased productivity, operational efficiency, security efficacy, and greater flexibility to innovate Conducting discovery to understand and articulate the key technical, operational, and commercial imperatives of your prospects and customers Working closely with Professional Services, Customer Success and Specialist teams to ensure overall customer implementation and adoption of solutions Demonstrating strong communication skills, influencing through effective presentations and customer-specific demos, and conducts technical engagements and workshops that are clear and impactful, simplifying complex ideas for various audiences Leading successful technical validation efforts based on best practices to ensure technical win in assigned opportunities Demonstrates Cross functional leadership driving collaboration and orchestrating supporting resources (Specialists, Channel Resources, Customer Support) to ensure a one-team approach that demonstrates a cohesive strategy Promoting end-to-end solutions that include PANW and/or partner professional services to ensure customers realize business value sooner Understanding the competitive landscape and effectively differentiating PANW's leadership in the cybersecurity space Continuously investing in yourself to develop technical and professional skills that drive your ever-increasing contributions to success of our customers while actively participating within the Solutions Consultant community and at industry events Identifying technical stakeholders and cultivating relationships with key personas to build and drive a security architecture transformation roadmap
   

  

**Qualifications**
 Required Qualifications 6+ years experience in pre-sales/sales engineering Skilled in at least one of the following Networking, Network Security, Cybersecurity, Private/Public Cloud Security, SOC/Endpoint or SASE Experience in delivering cybersecurity solutions that solve technical challenges and influence new business initiatives is preferred Influencing and gaining buy-in from key stakeholders, either in a customer-facing or internal role; prior experience in a pre-sales role is ideal Creating and delivering technical presentations, workshops, or technical validation engagements Experience in selling, designing, implementing, or managing one or more of the following solutions: Network Security, SASE, SaaS, CNAPP and/or SOC Transformation Technologies Partnering with Customer Support functions to ensure successful implementation and adoption of sold solutions Experience in complex sales involving long sales processes with multiple buying centers and multi-product solutions are preferred This is a field sales position where travel requirements may be required to support in person customer meetings, please discuss with the recruiter on the specifics for this position. Proficient in English and Swedish
   

  

**Our Commitment**
 We’re trailblazers that dream big, take risks, and challenge cybersecurity’s status quo. It’s simple: we can’t accomplish our mission without diverse teams innovating, together.
   

  

 We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for all qualified individuals with a disability. If you require assistance or accommodation due to a disability or special need, please contact us at accommodations@paloaltonetworks.com.
   

  

 Palo Alto Networks is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity in our workplace, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or other legally protected characteristics.
   

  

 All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.
   

  

 Is role eligible for Immigration Sponsorship? No. Please note that we will not sponsor applicants for work visas for this position.

title: Service Manager
description:
**Be visionary**
 Teledyne Technologies Incorporated provides enabling technologies for industrial growth markets that require advanced technology and high reliability. These markets include aerospace and defense, factory automation, air and water quality environmental monitoring, electronics design and development, oceanographic research, deepwater oil and gas exploration and production, medical imaging and pharmaceutical research.
   

  

 We are looking for individuals who thrive on making an impact and want the excitement of being on a team that wins.
   

  

**Job Description**
 Your contribution
   

  

 As Service Manager at FLIR Sweden AB (Täby), you will lead a skilled team of thermography service technicians and Customer Service Representatives (CSRs). Together, you will deliver repair, calibration, and restoration services that ensure exceptional customer experiences and uphold Teledyne FLIR’s reputation for reliability.
   

  

 This role is critical to our Service business: you will establish business goals, optimize resources, and contribute to policy and procedure development initiatives that drive growth. Your leadership will directly impact customer satisfaction, operational efficiency, and revenue performance, while fostering collaboration across regional teams and adjacent departments.
   

  

**Your job in plain text**
* Build and lead teams while developing processes to deliver outstanding customer service
* Establish and manage daily work priorities, delegating assignments and adjusting resources to meet business needs
* Oversee employee time and attendance, support conflict resolution, and foster adherence to company values
* Develop team members through coaching, one‑to‑one check‑ins, and annual performance appraisals
* Promote cross‑training and skill development to enhance capacity with existing resources
* Manage administration of all service activities using SAP and related enterprise applications
* Apply business acumen to identify and promote service offerings that drive growth while maintaining cost controls
* Track and report monthly Key Performance Indicators; analyze trends and recommend process improvements
* Manage operating expenditures with a focus on cost reduction; provide input for annual budget submissions
* Ensure compliance with ISO Quality standards and Global Trade Compliance policies
* Coordinate with adjacent departments to ensure timely delivery of products and services
* Foster collaboration within the regional Service group and identify opportunities for improvement


**To be ready to take on this role we would love if you have**
* Bachelor’s degree in electronics technology or a related discipline
* Significant experience in thermography service with expert knowledge of advanced thermal imaging systems
* Extensive leadership experience with direct report responsibility
* Experience working within an ISO 9001 environment
* Strong understanding of Global Trade Compliance policies and best practices
* Familiarity with Jurisdiction Classification processes
* Hands‑on experience with SAP PR1 Customer Service Module and ticketing platforms
* Proven ability to analyze requirements and develop processes/policies to support new product introductions
* Experience in developing and managing operating budgets with a focus on cost control
* In‑depth knowledge of metrology and calibration laboratory operations for thermal imaging products, compliant with NIST regulations


**What Teledyne FLIR Offers You**
* A leadership role where you shape and develop a high‑performing Service team
* The chance to work with advanced thermal imaging technology and contribute to world‑class repair and calibration services
* Direct impact on customer satisfaction and business growth through service excellence
* Collaboration with Product Management, Engineering, Manufacturing and other functions as well as regional Service teams to drive continuous improvement
* Opportunities to influence service strategy, processes, and policy development within a global organization
* A supportive culture that values teamwork, accountability, and professional development
* Competitive compensation and benefits, including wellness rewards and career advancement opportunities


**Interested?**
 If you liked what you just read, and believe you are the person that will make us better, do not hesitate to apply.
   

  

 The selection process is ongoing, so don't wait.
   

  

 When applying, make it easy for yourself. Add a resume and fill out the required fields, no need to add anything else at this point.
   

  

 We accept applications in English.
   

  

 (Non-US) This position requires access to export-controlled information or items (i.e. regulated technology or technical data per ITAR/EAR US regulations). Applicants must be eligible to apply for and obtain the appropriate export control license from the U.S. Departments of State or Commerce.
   

  

 or or
   

  

 Teledyne and all of our employees are committed to conducting business with the highest ethical standards. We require all employees to comply with all applicable laws, regulations, rules and regulatory orders. Our reputation for honesty, integrity and high ethics is as important to us as our reputation for making innovative sensing solutions.

title: Junior affärsorienterad systemkonsult
description:
På Decerno tror vi att framtidens systemutveckling kommer att se annorlunda ut än den gör idag. Vi ser en utveckling där kodning i allt större utsträckning automatiseras och där AI samt moderna utvecklingsplattformar förändrar hur affärskritiska system skapas, vidareutvecklas och förvaltas.
   

  

 För oss handlar framtidens systemutveckling mindre om manuell kodproduktion och mer om att förstå verksamheter, analysera behov, utforma smarta lösningar och skapa verkligt värde tillsammans med kund. Därför söker vi nu två juniora konsulter som vill vara med på den resan, som inleds med ett summer internship hos vårt systerbolag Genus i Oslo.
   

  

 Både Decerno och Genus är en del av Addnode Group, och ett nära samarbete har vuxit fram mellan bolagen – med fokus på att kombinera kompetenser och skapa ännu större värde för kunder.
   

  

**Om Decerno**
 Vi på Decerno är specialister på skräddarsydda digitala lösningar och är stolta över att våra uppdrag bidrar till samhällsnytta.
   

  

 Det betyder att vi förstår att alla kunder är olika och har unika förutsättningar. Vi skräddarsyr våra lösningar efter kundens behov vilket medför att våra medarbetare får möjlighet att arbeta i varierande och spännande projekt.
   

  

 Samtidigt som vi skapar digitala lösningar vill vi också göra skillnad på riktigt. Det är inte bara något vi säger, vi menar det! Sedan vår start 1984 har vår etiska policy varit vår kompass och vi väljer aktivt bort projekt inom vapenindustri, spel, tobak och alkohol.
   

  

**Din start – summer internship hos Genus i Oslo**
 Din anställning inleds med ett sju veckor långt summer internship hos Genus i Oslo (22 juni - 7e augusti), där du tillsammans med andra deltagare och erfarna kollegor lär dig plattformen och konsultrollen i praktiken.
   

  

*Under ditt internship kommer du att:*
* Arbeta i par med andra deltagare och växa genom nära samarbete i projektform
* Lära dig att modellera och konfigurera lösningar i Genus plattform
* Arbeta i ett riktigt kundprojekt med handledning och stöd av erfarna kollegor
* Förstå hur en konsult rör sig mellan kundens verksamhetsbehov och tekniska möjligheter


 Vi står självklart för boende och traktamente under veckorna i Oslo. Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning med sex månaders provanställning.
   

  

**Efter sommaren**
 Efter internshipet fortsätter du din anställning hos Decerno i Stockholm. Här blir du en del av våra kundteam och arbetar vidare i projekt där du får bidra med analys, struktur, kommunikation och systemförståelse.
   

  

 Dina arbetsuppgifter kan till exempel innebära att du:
   

  

* kartlägger verksamhetsprocesser och identifierar förbättringsmöjligheter
* fångar upp och konkretiserar kunders behov
* omsätter krav och processer till lösningar i Genus plattform
* arbetar med datamodellering, informationsstruktur och logik
* är delaktig i integrationer mellan olika system
* tar fram rapporter, analysstöd och beslutsunderlag
* bidrar till att skapa användarvänliga och ändamålsenliga gränssnitt
* samarbetar tätt med kunder, kollegor och andra kompetenser i projekt


 Över tid kan dina uppdrag variera beroende på kundernas behov, men rollen har alltid sin grund i att skapa värde i skärningspunkten mellan verksamhet och teknik.
   

  

**Är det här du?**
* du tar examen under våren 2026 inom exempelvis industriell ekonomi, systemvetenskap eller liknande
* är analytisk och strukturerad
* du trivs i dialog med kunder och kollegor
* har förståelse för verksamhetsprocesser och system
* är nyfiken på AI, no-code/low-code och framtidens systemutveckling
* talar och skriver flytande på svenska och engelska


**Det är extra positivt om du har:**
* erfarenhet av no-code/low-code, modellbaserad utveckling eller liknande plattformar
* praktik eller tidigare arbete inom IT, konsulting eller verksamhetsutveckling
* förståelse för kravarbete, processkartläggning eller systemförvaltning
* grundläggande kunskaper i exempelvis SQL eller Python
* intresse för affärsanalys, verksamhetsstyrning och digital transformation
* nyfikenhet på hur AI förändrar utveckling och verksamhetssystem


**Därför ska du välja Decerno**
 I våra medarbetarundersökningar blir det gång på gång tydligt att det är de fantastiska kollegorna, de roliga och utmanande projekten och möjligheten att bidra till samhällsnytta som gör att våra medarbetare trivs så bra hos oss.
   

  

 Sedan finns det också några ytterligare aspekter vilka vi ser som självklarheter:
   

  

* En flexibel arbetsplats med större frihet att planera vardagen för att få arbete och privatliv att gå ihop
* Extra föräldraförmåner eftersom vi tycker att familjen är viktigast
* Vi tycker att din kompetensutveckling är viktig! Därför anordnar vi interna utbildningar löpande i vår egen Dec Academy, gör satsningar inom AI och arrangerar årliga konferenser


 Utöver detta erbjuder vi även ett brett förmånspaket som inkluderar bland annat friskvårdsbidrag och ett generöst försäkringspaket.
   

  

**Låter detta som ditt nästa steg i karriären?**
 Vad kul! Då ser vi fram emot att läsa din ansökan och dina betyg så snart som möjligt.
   

  

**AI i rekryteringsprocessen**
 För att förbättra vår rekryteringsprocess använder vi AI-stöd. Alla ansökningar granskas alltid av våra rekryterare. Vi låter aldrig AI fatta egna beslut. Du har alltid rätt att få mer information om hur vi använder AI. Kontakta oss via rekrytering@decerno.se vid frågor.

title: Senior Infrastructure Architect & Team Lead
description:
**About The Company**
 A specialist in identity, secure transactions, and digital services, IN Groupe is the global leader in secure identity and trust services. Every day, our 4,000 employees across 40 countries work to ensure that everyone has access to a reliable and secure identity, both in the physical and digital worlds. With our solutions and our international network of 10 R&D centers, we support governments and businesses in more than 130 countries.
   

  

 Joining IN Groupe means helping to shape the future of the identity and secure transaction market. Become part of our international teams — driven by innovation and proud to design solutions that make life easier and protect personal data for millions of people around the world.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As
 **System Architect,** 
 you will act as the technical lead for the
 **Infrastructure & Tooling** 
 team in day-to-day operational matters while also owning the architectural direction of our SaaS service platforms.
   

  

 You will work at the intersection of
 **operations and architecture** 
 — ensuring stability and performance today, while designing scalable, secure, and automated platforms for tomorrow.
   

  

 This role requires deep
 **technical expertise,** 
 strong
 **leadership capabilities,** 
 and the ability to balance operational excellence with long-term architectural thinking.
   

  

**Main Responsabilities**
* Act as team lead for operational, day-to-day infrastructure topics and staffing related tasks.
* Define and evolve the architecture of our SaaS service platforms based on the IT OPS guidelines
* Drive infrastructure automation and Infrastructure-as-Code initiatives
* Ensure high availability, scalability, and security across our platforms
* Design and maintain Linux-based environments (primarily RHEL-based distributions)
* Collaborate with development, security, and product teams to align platform capabilities with business needs
* Contribute to continuous improvement of operational processes, reliability, and resilience
* Support strategic decisions regarding infrastructure technologies and roadmap


**About You**
* Very strong proficiency in Ansible and Terraform
* Solid experience with OpenStack (or similar private cloud platforms)
* Advanced knowledge of Linux environments (RHEL-based)
* Strong understanding of:
* Server infrastructure
* Storage solutions
* Networking
* Hardware Security Modules (HSMs)
* Experience designing and operating production-grade SaaS infrastructure
* Prior experience managing a team
* Comfortable working in security-sensitive and compliance-driven environments
* Professional proficiency in Swedish and English


**Nice-to-Have**
* Experience from regulated industries (eID, PKI, digital identity, financial services, public sector)
* Experience with high-availability architectures and geo-redundant designs
* Experience leading or mentoring other engineers


**Personal Qualities**
* A natural technical leader who enjoys guiding others in operational excellence
* Structured and analytical, with strong problem-solving skills
* Comfortable taking ownership of both daily operations and long-term strategy
* Passionate about automation, security, and resilient system design
* A team player who communicates clearly and collaborates across functions


**Background check**
 Transparency, security, and trust are the core of our company; therefore, the final candidate will be subjected to a background check as a mandatory step in the recruitment process.

title: Java Backend Developer
description:

title: Head of Audit for the CRO, CFO and Group Compliance Team | SEB, Stockholm
description:
Group Internal Audit (GIA) is an independent unit that reports directly to SEB’s Board of Directors via the Group Audit and Compliance Committee. The purpose of the function is to evaluate and give assurance to the Board and the President that governance, risk management and internal control processes as designed and represented by management are adequate and effective and support the business objectives. The department is truly global and consists of approximately 95 professionals that utilizes a common global methodology and audit tools.
   

  

 Since SEB was founded in 1856, our focus on customers and long-term relationships has been at the heart of our success. We aim to drive positive change by providing capital to help everyone reach their goals and accelerate progress towards a sustainable tomorrow. As a leading financial services group in northern Europe, we'll help you grow and empower you to take on more responsibility to create a real difference. Welcome to SEB.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As Head of Audit for the CRO, CFO and Group Compliance team, you will have main stakeholder relationship responsibility for all audit work conducted within the Control functions area CRO, Group Compliance and additionally also covering the CFO function of SEB.
   

  

 The role involves leading and managing a team of nine professional auditors located in Stockholm and Vilnius that conduct the audit work and to proactively manage audit relationships with the stakeholders. The tasks include leading the Risk Assessment process and ensuring that the risk- and regulatory based Audit Plan is delivered on time and with high quality. Also, you will oversee the audit process to ensure that the audit assignments comply with the established Global Audit Methodology, and the internal processes and systems. You will ensure proper allocation of resources and maximise the efficiency of the audit work conducted by the team.
   

  

 The Head of the Audit team is also responsible for providing quarterly reports on the audit work conducted in different areas to the Banks CFO, CRO, and Head of Group Compliance, senior management and to the Head of GIA. In this role it will also be required to ensure the quality of ad hoc investigations or support with requests from Financial Supervisory Authorities as approved by the Head of GIA. You are also a key stakeholder participating in interactions with regulators, mainly Finansinspektionen but also with SEB External Auditors.
   

  

**To thrive in this role, we believe you have**
 This is a multidimensional job which requires good leadership skills and the ability to understand and assess the big picture without losing attention to details. You are excellent in maintaining and building relationships within the organisation and to external stakeholders, but at the same time keep your professional integrity and act as a role model. The right candidate will have both strong leadership and excellent communication skills to be able to communicate with senior management but also on a detailed and more technical level.
   

  

 You will be part of the management team in GIA and it is required in that capacity to have the best interest for SEB in your mind. In addition, we look for an individual with a broad knowledge of the banking industry and good overall understanding of the regulatory environment SEB operates in. You have experience from working with questions pertaining to regulatory models and capital requirements as well as emerging non-financial risk areas. The area has become highly complex over the years with a mix of regulatory expectation but at the same time manage to focus on a risk-based audit plan.
   

  

 In addition;
   

  

* You have a minimum of 10 years of working experience in the banking industry or similar, preferably with an external or internal audit-, supervisory or quantitative background
* You have documented management skills and practical experience to coach and develop a team of senior experts and quantitative analysts
* Knowledge in areas pertaining to capital requirements and regulatory models is of merit
* You are curious, have high integrity and enjoy leading teams with mixed skills and backgrounds in project type assignments
* Senior expertise in business areas/functions and understanding of the regulatory requirements in the banking sector
* You are an excellent communicator with the ability to build trust and relationships at all levels in the SEB organisation
* You have applicable university degree and fluent in English (both verbally and written language)
* You are truly interested in how you can apply AI/Digital tools/data analytics in conducting the audit work


**What We Offer**
 This position will provide the jobholder with a strong possibility to further develop the existing team of professionals to help them achieve their potential and advance their own career by taking on an important role within GIA.
   

  

 We offer many experiences and benefits to our employees, and there is nuance to every individual’s career experience, but the elements that define the core of our offering are:
   

  

* Extensive training and learning opportunities
* Friendly and welcoming culture
* International opportunities and working environment
* Agile and modern ways of working


**Do you want to be part of SEB?**
 It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.
   

  

 Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today via the link in this ad, but no later than May 14th, 2026. If you have questions about the position, please contact Björn Rosenqvist, Head of Group Internal Audit, at bjorn.rosenkvist@seb.se or Madeleine Gustâv, Talent Acquisition Partner, at madeleine.gustav@seb.se, but we will not process applications via email.
   

  

 Please be aware that our final candidates undergo background checks, a process that includes for example identity control, verification of qualifications, credit checks, company engagements and criminal records checks. In some cases, we also apply random drug checks. During employment, employees within SEB can also expect to undergo recurring background checks.
   

  

 SEB Sweden has a redeployment responsibility, why this position might be covered by internal redeployment.
   

  

 Learn more about working at SEB on our Career website or through our Career podcasts.
   

  

 sebgroup.com/career
   

  

 bit.ly/SEBcareer

title: Product Owner – D365
description:
**Description: Product Owner – D365 (with Business Analyst responsibilities)** 




  





**Scope:** 




 - Ownership of Microsoft Dynamics 365 (D365) within an assigned functional area (e.g., Order-to-Delivery)
 



 - Cross-functional collaboration with IT, business stakeholders, and external partners
 



 - Contribution to ways of working, standardization, and continuous improvement initiatives
 



 - Monitoring and follow-up of relevant KPIs linked to processes and system support About the
 



  





**Role:** 




 The Product Owner – D365 is responsible for owning, prioritizing, and developing functionality within Microsoft Dynamics 365 (D365) for a defined process area.
 



 The role ensures that D365 solutions effectively support business needs, are standardized where applicable, and deliver measurable business value.
 



 This position combines strategic product ownership with hands-on Business Analyst responsibilities, bridging business requirements and technical delivery.
 



  





**Key responsibilities: Product Ownership:** 




 - Own and manage the product vision and roadmap for D365 within the assigned domain
 



 - Ensure that development initiatives and changes align with business objectives and architectural principles
 



 - Prioritize and maintain the product backlog based on business value, risks, and dependencies
 



 - Define scope and make decisions on functional priorities
 



 - Act as the primary interface between business stakeholders and development teams
 



 - Ensure solutions are scalable and compliant with established standards Business Analyst
 



  





**Responsibilities:** 




 - Analyze and document current and future-state business processes
 



 - Elicit, structure, and document business requirements
 



 - Translate business needs into user stories and functional specifications
 



 - Facilitate workshops and requirements gathering sessions
 



 - Support solution design in collaboration with architects and development teams
 



 - Contribute to test planning, execution, and acceptance activities
 



 - Ensure traceability between requirements, delivered solutions, and business outcomes
 



  





**Required qualifications: Experience:** 




 - Proven experience as a Product Owner, Business Analyst, or in a similar role
 



 - Experience working within ERP environments
 



 - Hands-on experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365
 



 - Experience in requirements management and process modeling
 



 - Experience working in agile environments
 



  





**Knowledge:** 




 - Strong understanding of end-to-end business processes, preferably within Order-to-Delivery
 



 - Good understanding of system architecture and integration principles
 



 - Ability to analyze data and track performance through KPIs
 



  





**Skills:** 




 - Strong analytical and structured approach to work -
 



 Excellent communication skills
 



 - Ability to prioritize effectively and make informed decisions
 



 - Strong stakeholder management capabilities
 



  





**Personal profile:** 




 - Structured and analytical mindset
 



 - Strong communication and interpersonal skills
 



 - Ability to prioritize and make decisions in a dynamic environment
 



 - Proven ability to manage and engage stakeholders effectively

title: Product Manager, Courier Compliance
description:
**About Wolt**
 At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
   

  

 Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
   

  

 We are looking for a Product Manager to join
 **the Courier Group, Compliance Team.** 
 The Courier Group builds products that power the end-to-end courier experience and logistics at Wolt, from onboarding to earnings, engagement, task assignment, and compliance. The group spans multiple product teams working together to ensure that couriers can start, operate, and grow on Wolt safely, efficiently, and in compliance with local regulations.
   

  

 The Compliance Team ensures that Wolt’s courier platform operates within the boundaries of diverse regulatory environments, while maintaining a simple and trusted experience for couriers, third-party service providers, and internal teams. This includes identity verification, document management, classification, privacy safeguards, and scalable compliance automation.
   

  

 What You’ll Do
   

  

 As the Product Manager for Compliance, you’ll work with a cross-functional team of exceptional engineers, designers, analysts, and product people in a high-impact, high-visibility role. You’ll define the vision and roadmap for courier compliance at Wolt, balancing business scalability, user trust, and regulatory integrity. You’ll connect with country teams to understand local regulatory requirements and cultural nuances, collaborate with legal and operations partners, and align with other product teams to make sure compliance flows are seamlessly embedded across the courier lifecycle.
   

  

 Your work will include leading multiple projects and cross-team initiatives across the product lifecycle, from discovery to delivery. You’ll partner with Legal, Operations, and Policy teams to design compliance systems that are both locally adaptive and globally scalable. You’ll ensure that product experiences for different courier models remain clearly distinct and compliant, while driving automation and efficiency in ID verification, document management, and audit workflows. A key part of your role will be developing a deep understanding of couriers, regulators, and internal users, enabling you to build solutions that make compliance seamless and effortless for everyone involved.
   

  

 Our Humble Expectations
   

  

 You could come to this role from a variety of backgrounds, but ideally, you have:
   

  

* Demonstrated experience as Product Manager, preferably within B2C product domains
* Excellent communication skills, with the ability to influence cross-functional stakeholders and simplify complex legal or technical topics.
* A mix of analytical ability, technical understanding, and business judgment — with the go-getter mindset of a “start-up CEO.”
* A strong track record of working with and coaching cross-functional teams, including engineers, data analysts, finance, legal, and ops.
* Experience building globally scalable systems that adapt to local market variations.


 Next Steps
   

  

 The position will be filled as soon as we find the right person, so make sure to apply as soon as you realize you really, really want to join us!
   

  

 The compensation will be a negotiable combination of monthly pay and DoorDash RSUs. The latter makes it exceptionally easy to be excited about our company growing and doing well, as you’ll own a piece of the pie.
   

  

 Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
   

  

 We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

title: Leveranstekniker till Ledande IT-aktör
description:
Här får du möjlighet att bli en del av ett engagerat team som arbetar med moderna och effektiva IT-lösningar inom offentlig sektor där kvalitet, säkerhet och långsiktiga lösningar står i centrum. Du kommer att ha en nyckelroll i tekniska leveranser som säkerställer driftsäkra och robusta IT-miljöer för verksamheter med höga krav på tillgänglighet.
 



  





**Om tjänsten:** 




 Som leveranstekniker ansvarar du för att implementera och leverera IT-lösningar till kundens verksamhet. Du arbetar huvudsakligen i OnPrem- och Windowsmiljöer via fjärruppkoppling, där du säkerställer att allt fungerar smidigt och enligt plan. Rollen innebär ett varierat arbete som omfattar både självständiga uppdrag och samarbete med kollegor och andra intressenter.
 



  





**Arbetsuppgifter:** 



* Installera och konfigurera applikationer i OnPrem-miljöer
* Vara delaktig i införanden och uppgraderingar
* Felsöka och hantera driftsrelaterade problem
* Hjälpa kunder vidare i sin IT-miljö
* Dokumentera lösningar och konfigurationer



  





**För att lyckas i rollen tror vi att du uppfyller något av följande:** 



* Några års erfarenhet som IT-tekniker inom OnPrem- och Windowsmiljöer
* God kunskap om Windows Server (2016, 2019 och senare)
* God erfarenhet av PowerShell och scripting
* Vana av Git och/eller TFS
* Kunskap inom backup och säkerhet i OnPrem-miljöer
* Arbetat med hybridlösningar, exempelvis Azure eller Microsoft 365
* Goda kunskaper i svenska, både i tal och skrift



  





**Meriterande:** 



* Erfarenhet av Active Directory, DNS, DHCP och andra nätverksfunktioner
* Goda kunskaper inom virtualisering, t.ex. Hyper-V eller VMware



 Här får du arbeta i en tekniskt mogen miljö där långsiktighet och kvalitet värderas högt. Rollen passar dig som gillar att kombinera problemlösning med tekniska leveranser och som vill vara med och utveckla stabila IT-miljöer med höga krav på säkerhet och tillgänglighet.
 



  





**Om företaget:** 




 Ett etablerat bolag inom system- och IT-lösningar för offentlig sektor, med fokus på långsiktiga kundrelationer och värdeskapande tekniska leveranser.
